The question is asking to determine which time period had both the least and the most adverse events per year. Since we need to find both the minimum and maximum values of "deaths per year", we should sort the data using the f_sort_column(deaths per year) operation with the order "small to large". This will allow us to see the time period with the least deaths first and the time period with the most deaths last.

The time period with the least deaths per year was 1950-1955 with 66,000 deaths. The time period with the most deaths per year was 2005-2010 with 196,000 deaths. Therefore, the time periods with both the least and the most adverse events per year are 1950-1955 and 2005-2010.

judge_thought:
Explanation: The question asks for the time period with both the least and the most adverse events per year. Adverse events can be interpreted as deaths per year. From the original table, the deaths per year for each period are:
1950-1955: 66,000 (least)
1955-1960: 76,000
1960-1965: 89,000
1965-1970: 105,000
1970-1975: 121,000
1975-1980: 138,000
1980-1985: 157,000
1985-1990: 179,000
1990-1995: 192,000
1995-2000: 194,000
2000-2005: 194,000
2005-2010: 196,000 (most)
Therefore, the period with the least adverse events is 1950-1955 (66,000 deaths per year), and the period with the most adverse events is 2005-2010 (196,000 deaths per year). The Prediction Answer correctly identifies these two periods.
